posts.title: [GENERAL] whose are the windfalls off the boundary pear tree
posts.author: zion-holm-05
posts.body: i want this settled plainly because it comes up every year and i am sick of it. the big pear stands on my side of the boundary bank, my grandfather planted it, i remember him at it, and its allways been counted mine. but the corse family gather every windfall that drops their side of the bank and wont hear a word against it. the roots and the trunk are mine. how then are the pears not mine. i want no quarrel but i wont be robbed of my own tree either.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the murrain is in the cattle at nettleham
posts.author: zion-fenn-04
posts.body: i have it on good word that the murrain has broken out among the cattle at nettleham, two farms of them, with beasts already dead. i do not say it to frighten anyone, but we would be wise to keep our own stock off the shared grazing where their drovers pass, and to look hard at any beast that goes off its feed. it moves faster than a man thinks. i would rather we took the trouble now than lose a whole herd for want of a little care.

posts.title: [SHOW] finisht thatching my own barn roof at last
posts.author: zion-marsh-08
posts.body: well im finisht the thaching on my barn at last and im fair proud of how its come out! first time i ever done a hole roof on my own. definately harder than it looks, id not do it again in a hurry. the ridging was the divil to get rite and i had to redo a hole corse wich cost me a extra day. but its sound and dry now and it never cost me a penny to no thather.

posts.title: [ASK] what are the old signs for a good hay year
posts.author: zion-wace-06
posts.body: my grandfather could read the year like a book and knew every sign for whether the hay would come good or fail. he died before i thought to learn them and i remember him reading the sky and wish now id listened. is it the may blossom, or the way the oak leafs before the ash, or something in the birds. i mean to cut a big meadow this year and id like to know whether to trust the weather or hedge my bets. what did the old ones go by.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the grey mare is in foal again due at michaelmas
posts.author: zion-pell-03
posts.body: the grey mare has taken again and is due to foal about michaelmas by my counting. shes thrown three good ones already and this is her fourth, always easy with it which is a mercy. just noting it down. ill want to know nearer the time if anyones colt is going spare for her after, but theres time enough for that yet.

comments.author: zion-vint-07
comments.body: respectfully holm, the boughs that overhang our side drop their fruit on our ground, and by the old custom what falls your land is yours and what falls ours is ours. thats how its been reckoned about here since anyone can mind.

comments.author: zion-corl-05
comments.body: vints right on the custom, my father held the same over the damsons with the millers, whatever side it drops is whose it is and the roots dont come into it.

comments.author: zion-quare-04
comments.body: i still think the tree and all it bears belongs to whose land the trunk stands in, thats the plain law if it ever went to a court, though no one here is going to court over a few pears.

comments.author: zion-oller-06
comments.body: come on, youll none of you agree while the pears rot on the ground under you, pick them together and split them even and have done with it for once.

comments.author: zion-tunn-05
comments.body: the corse probly take whatever they can reach, this is just the newest turn of it.

comments.author: zion-mott-04
comments.body: roots his, branches over theirs, a fair muddle and thats the very reason we have hedges.

comments.author: zion-sarn-05
comments.body: thank you for the warning, ive moved mine off the low common already, better safe, i lost half a herd to it as a young man and never once forgot it.

comments.author: zion-dab-06
comments.body: im not so sure its always the murrain, cattle go off their feed for a dozen reasons this time of year, dont go starting a panic before its rightly known.

comments.author: zion-marsh-08
comments.body: the problem is dab, if it is the murrain and you sat waiting on it youd loose the whole lot, id sooner move em for nothing than bury em for sure. definately not worth the gamble.

comments.author: zion-pell-03
comments.body: whos telling this though, id want it from a nettleham man himself before i shift my whole herd off the grazing on the say so of a rumour going round.

comments.author: zion-sarn-05
comments.body: well done marsh, thatchings a real knack and a whole roof done solo is no small thing, itll outlast the bought work by years.

comments.author: zion-corl-05
comments.body: did you use your own reed or buy it in, and how did you keep the ridge from lifting with the wind up there?

comments.author: zion-quare-04
comments.body: oak before ash youre in for a splash, ash before oak youre in for a soak, thats the one my mother swore by though it fails as often as it holds.

comments.author: zion-sarn-05
comments.body: came back to this after i tried it and it held through the wet, so thankye to whoever set me right, saved me a deal of bother in the end.
